title: dryas octopetala f. lanata
intro: Dryas octopetala f. lanata, also known as the white dryad or white mountain avens, is a flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. Its health benefits include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and soothing digestive issues. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
